title: WHO Confirms 64 Killed in Al Daein Hospital Strike; International Condemnation Mounts
detail: WHO and international organizations formally confirmed that the March 20 (Eid al-Fitr) strike on Al Daein Teaching Hospital killed at least 64 people — including 13 children, 7 pregnant women, 2 nurses, and 1 doctor — with 89 wounded. Yale Humanitarian Research Lab satellite analysis confirmed at least three distinct strike points from precision aerial munitions in a double-tap attack, with two strikes occurring approximately 20 minutes apart. The hospital, the only fully operational referral hospital in East Darfur, was rendered non-functional, leaving over 2 million people without access to specialized healthcare. The RSF blamed the SAF; SAF denied the attack but two military officials suggested the intended target was a nearby police station. UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk described the strike as 'appalling.' The attack brought total war deaths from attacks on healthcare facilities to over 2,000.

media.caption: WHO confirms 64 killed in Al Daein hospital strike, international condemnation follows

title: RSF Drone Strikes Ad Dabbah, Northern State; 6 Killed, Electricity Infrastructure Damaged
detail: An RSF drone struck Ad Dabbah, a town in Northern State on the Nile River, killing at least 6 people. The strike damaged the engineering college and an electricity substation, causing widespread power outages across the area. The attack on Ad Dabbah — deep inside SAF-controlled northern Sudan — demonstrated the RSF's extended drone reach beyond frontline areas and was part of an ongoing pattern of RSF targeting power and civilian infrastructure in government-held territory. It occurred on the same day as international outrage over the Al Daein hospital strike.

media.caption: RSF drone strikes Ad Dabbah in Northern State, killing 6
